Off Calendar Oct-13-2017 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Defendant City & County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication (Added For The Purpose Of Entering Tentative Ruling) is off calendar as a duplicate entry. (302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-13-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Amy D. Super, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3931, for defendant and moving party;
Dean Royer, Esq. of the Law Office of Dean Royer, Tel. 415.795.1475, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling re DEFENDANT CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication as follows: Defendant City and County of San Francisco and Leslie Dubbin's motion for summary judgment is denied and their alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first cause of action for sexual harassment and granted as to the second cause of action for retaliation in violation of FEHA and the Labor Code. As to the sexual harassment claim, defendants failed to satisfy their burden of production. Defendants' undisputed material facts 77-84 show that Ms. Dubbin singled out plaintiff Cheryl Denson-Thornton for a birthday party, talked about her uterus, brushed up against her, looked her up and down, sidled up to her and stuck her hands between Ms. Dubbin's thighs. A reasonable trier of fact could conclude that this conduct committed by a supervisor was sufficiently severe or pervasive to constitute actionable harassment. As to the retaliation claim, the City satisfied its burden that Ms. Denson-Thornton did not suffer any adverse employment action because of her protected activity and Ms. Denson-Thornton failed to provide any evidence creating a triable dispute on that issue. Defendants' Facts 1-76 and 85-133 show that the retaliation claims lacks merit. Some of the retaliatory conduct complained of by Ms. Denson-Thornton does not constitute an adverse employment. (See, e.g., Facts 100-106 and 108 (Blum's written warnings re the performance appraisal and e-mails); Fact 107 (seating arrangement); Fact 123 (meeting).) While the failure to promote is an adverse employment action, Ms. Denson-Thornton acknowledges that she was unqualified for some of the positions she sought (Facts 32-35) and there were legitimate reasons why she did not obtain some of the other positions (Fact 30). Ms. Denson-Thornton's main allegation of retaliation is that she worked out of class, had extensive administrative experience, and Ms. Dubbin altered her performance appraisal review (PAR) to misrepresent her experience, and that misrepresentation caused her not to obtain the jobs. (Second Amended Complaint, par. 26.) There is insufficient evidence to support this "cat's paw" theory of retaliation. Ms. Denson-Thornton cites her rejection of the 2593 classification position. Cathy Abela's declaration, which Ms. Denson-Thornton does not dispute, shows that the hiring manager at the location makes the hiring decisions (Facts 18-21) and the hiring manager did not fill the 2593 position because he has been on leave (Fact 27). For the 2588 positions, Ms. Denson-Thornton declined one interview because it was for a temporary position and the other position had incumbents and the interview was a mere formality (Fact 30; Denson-Thornton Dec. par. 39.) There is no evidence that Ms. Denson-Thornton's PAR had any role in these decisions. Ms. Denson-Thornton complains that Albert Yu (Ms. Dubbin's supervisor) retaliated against her because he discussed transferring her. (Facts 91 and 93.) Ms. Denson-Thornton was never transferred, however. (Fact 118.) Ms. Denson-Thornton also argues that she has been losing her work duties, but that factual theory is not alleged in the second amended complaint and thus cannot be the basis to deny summary adjudication.

Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which complies with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes Hunter CSR #6576 email: rhunter@sftc.org. (302)
